heading: Uniform Federal Crime Reporting Act of 1988
label: 41303 Uniform Federal Crime Reporting Act of 1988
notes: CodificationSection was formerly classified as a note under , Judiciary and Judicial Procedure, prior to editorial reclassification and renumbering as this section.section 534 of Title 28

Amendments2018—Subsec. (c)(3). , substituted “not less frequently than annually” for “in the form of annual Uniform Crime Reports for the United States”.Pub. L. 115–393, § 402(1)Subsec. (c)(4), (5). , added pars. (4) and (5).Pub. L. 115–393, § 402(2)

children.heading: Short title
children.label: a Short title
children.text: This section may be cited as the “Uniform Federal Crime Reporting Act of 1988”.

children.heading: Definitions
children.label: b Definitions
children.text: For purposes of this section, the term “Uniform Crime Reports” means the reports authorized under and administered by the Federal Bureau of Investigation which compiles nationwide criminal statistics for use in law enforcement administration, operation, and management and to assess the nature and type of crime in the United States.section 534 of title 28

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Attorney General shall acquire, collect, classify, and preserve national data on Federal criminal offenses as part of the Uniform Crime Reports.

children.heading: Reporting by Federal agencies
children.label: 2 Reporting by Federal agencies
children.text: All departments and agencies within the Federal government (including the Department of Defense) which routinely investigate complaints of criminal activity, shall report details about crime within their respective jurisdiction to the Attorney General in a uniform manner and on a form prescribed by the Attorney General. The reporting required by this subsection shall be limited to the reporting of those crimes comprising the Uniform Crime Reports.

children.heading: Distribution of data
children.label: 3 Distribution of data
children.text: The Attorney General shall distribute data received pursuant to paragraph (2), not less frequently than annually, to the President, Members of the Congress, State governments, and officials of localities and penal and other institutions participating in the Uniform Crime Reports program.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Not later than 90 days after , the Director of the Federal Bureau of Investigation shall coordinate with the head of each department or agency within the Federal Government that is subject to the mandatory reporting requirements under paragraph (2) for the purpose of ensuring successful implementation of paragraph (2).December 21, 2018

children.heading: For report
children.label: B For report
children.text: Not later than 6 months after , the head of each department or agency within the Federal Government that is subject to the mandatory reporting requirements under paragraph (2) shall provide the Director of the Federal Bureau of Investigation such information as the Director determines is necessary to complete the first report required under paragraph (5).December 21, 2018

children.heading: Annual report by Federal Bureau of Investigation
children.label: 5 Annual report by Federal Bureau of Investigation
children.text: Not later than 1 year after , and annually thereafter, the Director of the Federal Bureau of Investigation shall prepare and submit to the Committee on the Judiciary of the Senate and the Committee on the Judiciary of the House of Representatives a report detailing the efforts of the departments and agencies within the Federal Government to come into compliance with paragraph (2). The report shall contain a list of all departments and agencies within the Federal Government subject to paragraph (2) and whether each department or agency is in compliance with paragraph (2).December 21, 2018

children.heading: Role of Federal Bureau of Investigation
children.label: d Role of Federal Bureau of Investigation
children.text: The Attorney General may designate the Federal Bureau of Investigation as the lead agency for purposes of performing the functions authorized by this section and may appoint or establish such advisory and oversight boards as may be necessary to assist the Bureau in ensuring uniformity, quality, and maximum use of the data collected.

children.heading: Inclusion of offenses involving illegal drugs
children.label: e Inclusion of offenses involving illegal drugs
children.text: The Director of the Federal Bureau of Investigation is authorized to classify offenses involving illegal drugs and drug trafficking as a part I crime in the Uniform Crime Reports.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: f Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated $350,000 for fiscal year 1989 and such sums as may be necessary to carry out the provisions of this section after fiscal year 1989.

children.heading: Effective date
children.label: g Effective date
children.text: The provisions of this section shall be effective on .January 1, 1989
